back_translation: For the choirmaster. To the tune of 'Muth-labben.' A psalm of David.
I will give thanks to the LORD with all my heart; I will recount all Your wonders.
I will be glad and rejoice in You, O Most High; I will sing praise to Your name.
When my enemies turn back, they stumble and perish before Your face.
For You have granted me justice and equity; You sit on Your throne, judging with righteousness.
You have rebuked the nations; You have destroyed the wicked; You have blotted out their name forever and ever.
The enemy has met his end, in everlasting ruins; You have uprooted their cities; even the memory of them has perished.
But the LORD abides forever; He has established His throne for judgment.
He will judge the world with righteousness; He governs the peoples with equity.
The LORD is a stronghold for the oppressed, a refuge in times of trouble.
Those who know Your name will trust in You, O LORD, for You have not forsaken those who seek You.
Sing praises to the LORD, who dwells in Zion; proclaim His deeds among the nations.
For He who avenges bloodshed remembers them; He does not forget the cry of the afflicted.
O LORD, be merciful to me; see the affliction I suffer from those who hate me, You who lift me up from the gates of death,
that I may declare all Your praises at the gates of Daughter Zion, and rejoice in Your salvation.
The nations have sunk into the pit they dug; their feet are caught in the net they hid.
The LORD is known by the justice He works; the wicked is snared by the work of his own hands. Higgaion Selah
The wicked will return to the realm of the dead, all the nations that forget God.
For the needy will not be forgotten forever, nor the hope of the afflicted perish for eternity.
Arise, O LORD, do not let man prevail; let the nations be judged before Your face.
O LORD, strike them with terror; let the nations know that they are but men. Selah
